The room was not cleaned or the beds made. When we inquired, we were told that we should have mentioned this at check-in. However, we were not informed of that. Given the high price paid, an uncleaned room is unacceptable!

title: It was supposed to be a rest after hiking in the Karkonosze Mountains😡
pos_text:
The hotel has a very good location. It is located in the very center. A few steps and you are in the Market Square. Breakfasts are tasty. Possibility to order scrambled eggs or fried eggs. Well served.
Delicious coffee.Efficient service.

Restaurant "Dwór Polski" has an entrance from the Market Square. Great location, service and delicious dishes. I recommend!!
neg_text:
The windows of my room faced the street. Terrible noise until morning - people, motorbikes and cars. After my hike in the Karkonosze Mountains I couldn't rest.
When I opened the window it fell off its hinges. On the floor there is a carpet that has seen its glory days. The dark brown furniture is meant to resemble a manor house. They are worn out.
I do not recommend a room with windows facing the street during the holidays.

title: Worst hotel in a long time
pos_text:
Breakfast was very tasty with freshly prepared scrambled eggs. The staff here were also very courteous.
The proximity to the city center is great.
neg_text:
The journey was difficult because the alleged parking spaces do not exist. Eight pieces for the entire hotel means we got nothing. Unfortunately, I only found out after booking.
The lady at the reception was unfriendly and spoke poor English and no German at all, which made communication difficult.
It's best to use the elevator only in an emergency, but it's still better than your room.
We were accommodated under the roof. It was uncomfortably warm, but the noise outside made it impossible to open a window. Every door squeaked, the beds creaked. We both had sore backs the next day; it was the most restless night of our entire vacation.
The bathroom is in keeping with the hotel, but the shower is too small for an adult and the water pressure is terrible.
There was also a useless television.

title: Compared to other 4-star hotels, this one performed very poorly.
pos_text: Great location, hotel service very nice,
neg_text: No air conditioning, instead a small fan, at 30 degrees. it didn't help much. Musty smell in the room.In the bathroom, the shower tray is sagging, the cistern is cracked, and the shelf under the mirror is poorly hung.Be careful, the elevator broke down once during our stay, /we lived on the 3rd floor/ Breakfast was very sparse, the absolute minimum.

title: Beautiful location. But really no comfort; after a long journey, I didn't get any rest due to the heat and noise.
pos_text: Friendly staff
neg_text: Rooms very warm. No air conditioning. Super old bathroom. A lot of noise from outside. Scraping car's, motors, and deafening noises from the kitchen and renovations next door.

title: Not worth 4 stars
pos_text: Close to the center
neg_text: The hotel definitely does not meet the 4 stars. The floor tiles in the bathroom are falling off, the shower head barely sticks to the wall, the hairdryer is so old (I thought they didn't exist anymore) it didn't work to dry your hair because it was all hot and you couldn't hold it in your hands. The elevator is terrible, breakfast was the same for all 4 days. We got a family suite with windows facing the courtyard, where a cafe is open in the evening. Since it was hot, we had to open the windows, so the noise was loud and we could only fall asleep after midnight. And the cafe cleaner would even think of playing music at 2 a.m. The bathroom door in the ceiling is pressed against the paper to keep it from falling, just like the tables where we ate breakfast are pressed against the paper.

title: Expensive, worn out but very central
pos_text: The location and good breakfast.
neg_text:
Worn but old carpet that doesn't smell fresh. The room needs a renovation. When the weather was hot, the room became like a sauna. The worst thing is that the street outside is used as a race track with terrible noise evening and night.
Clearly overpriced accommodation. Don't stay here.

title: Great location, but the car posers ruin it.
pos_text:
Good location
Friendly staff
neg_text:
Extremely loud.
Car posers on the street directly in front of the room until late into the night on a normal Thursday.
The hotel says they can't do anything; But if the hotels don't put pressure on the authorities, nothing will change.

title: One of a kind, unfortunately.
pos_text: Location is amazing. Breakfast is good. The staff prepared breakfast bag for us when we left for home. It was early in the morning at 4 am, so that was a nice and kind surprise.
neg_text:
Extremely noisy if you open the window during most of the night, every night. Not because of party, traffic, but because muscle and sports cars and motor cycles doing burn outs all the time and super heavy acceleration.

Super problematic because the room didnt have AC at all and with window open = no sleep.

More over, not one single day with room cleaning. At checkout i forgot to pay what was taken from the minibar, and they didnt bring it up either. This resulted in the fact that i will have to manually wire over money from home. Hotel staff chose not to disregard the small money despite admitting to the lack of room cleaning.

title: Beautiful city, bad rooms for the price!
pos_text: Central location and charm. Breakfast was okay!
neg_text: Did not receive the booked room, amenities not as described, no TV reception possible. Poor cushioning on the loungers. Broken lamps, a broken chair. Rooms 312 and 315 are impossibly poorly equipped! Very loud due to wannabe race car drivers! It's not worth the money; we wouldn't book this hotel again.

title: Wrocław city center for max 1 night!!!
pos_text: Location and staff
neg_text: Terrible breakfast, old, damaged equipment, decades old, no amenities whatsoever... it's a maximum of 2 stars and the description says 4 - laughable!!!

title: Poor
pos_text: Perfect location. Even though it's right next to the Market Square, the room is quiet. ,Delicious coffee.
neg_text: The standard of the 80s. The seat upholstery and carpet are dirty, the breakfast is stale. Where do these 4 stars come from?

title: Poor
pos_text: Very good location
neg_text:
I've been there twice already.
They each had different rooms on different floors. It was noisy every time, both from the street and from inside.
During the communist era, the hotel was a flagship hotel in Breslau. The interior design has not changed much since then.
The breakfast was very good.
So it's very suitable for young people who want to party in the city for a weekend, but not so much for people who want to visit Wrocław for a few days, explore it and find peace and quiet.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: We had a room facing the street; it was very noisy. The reception staff offered us earplugs.
neg_text: The street noise, cleanliness

title: Disappointing
neg_text: Keine Nachtruhe möglich da ab 22.00 Uhr bis 4.00 Uhr die Fußgängerzone/ Spielstraße zur Partymeile und Rennstrecke wird. Leider war das Personal nicht bemüht dieses wenigstens "versuchsweise" zu unterbinden. Das Frühstück war auch etwas " einfach".

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Great location
neg_text:
Hotel very tired and need TLC
Only some staff engaged with the resident's as spending to much time on their mobile. Brekfast below average

title: Mai più
pos_text: La posizione e l'acqua in camera offerta tutte le mattine
neg_text: La colazione immangiabile, le camere vecchie così come la struttura, letti scomodissimi, pulizia assente.

title: Disappointing
pos_text:
Emplacement pratique derrière la place centrale et l'hôtel de Ville.Entrree par une jolie cour pavée.
Très bon petit déjeuner.Personnel au petit déjeuner très accueillant et discret.
neg_text:
Chambre petite et encombrée par le mobilier rustique et sombre. Manque de climatisation.
Personnel a la réception courtois mais pas chaleureux.
Information concernant une remise de -10% au restaurant n'est pas respectée par le serveur.
Bon 3 étoiles et non 4 prétendu par l'établissement.
On a pas envie de revenir.

title: Disappointing
neg_text:
- I did not expect that the hotel parking for which I paid 70 PLN per day (approximately 16 EUR) would be on the next street, and in order to leave it, you had to bring a hotel employee with a card to open the barrier each time.
- I did not expect that the extra bed that I paid 90 PLN per night (approximately 20 EUR) would turn out to be a broken rollaway bed with missing springs and sagging to the floor, and another similar bed that it was changed to the next day would turn out to be sticking out of mattress with springs, on which it was impossible to lie for more than 5 minutes.

The next day, after a lengthy explanation about the ruined vacation, I was given a personal parking card, instead of the damaged beds, they gave me another room and a coupon for 50 PLN for lunch at the hotel restaurant.

title: Schöner Stadtbesuch, aber Lärm auf dem Hof und anliegendem Club bis in die Morgenstunden.
pos_text: Das Frühstück war sehr gut. Die Stadtlage des Hotels auch.
neg_text: Es war unerträglich laut jede Nacht. Für das Auto hatten wir täglich noch 90 Z loty zu zahlen, da es keine Hotel eigenen Parkplätze gibt (nur auf Vorbestellung und dann für 70 Zloty pro Tag). Dieser Umstand war vorher nicht klar beschrieben. Der Zimmerservice musste aufgefordert werden, sonst wäre er gar nicht gekommen. Kein zweites Mal werden wir dort sein.

title: Nie bylam zadowolona
pos_text: Sniadanie na wynos bardzo dobre
neg_text: Wąskie łóżka, mało gniazdek brak klimatyzacji, hałas

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Lokalizacja hotelu, ale nie lokalizacja pokoi!cudowna Pani Ania, recepcja....współczuje w tym upale obsługiwać gości, jest XIX wiek i klimatyzacja przy tych temperaturach to must, człowieku właścicielu hotelu....
neg_text: Brak klimatyzacji,NIEurozmaicone śniadania+kto pisał tę opinie na bookingu, kolega właściciela?, Ludzie mają prawo znać prawdę....)hałas od ulicy, hałas od dziedzińca-pokoje wychodzą na wąską uliczkę nad pubem dla pijanych Angoli, Iroli i innych gdzie wszystko niesie do góry do godz.1.00. W tygodniu, a w weekend.....to aż strach się bac (powinniście uprzedzać)....a spać przy zamkniętych oknach w pokoju bez klimy to pewna śmierć, było 32 stopni....,zasłony i firanki które trudno przesuwać...poduszki tak płaskie że ich wcale nie czuć pod głową....ciasne pokoje, hotel wymaga odświeżenia....i te sniadania....proszę was.... jajecznica z jajek w proszku,co to Mc Donalds....dzięki Wam była to ostatnia moja wizyta we Wroclove, bye

title: Emplacement géographiquement idéal, mais trop bruyant le soir et la nuit !
pos_text: Emplacement au coeur de la ville, idéal pour découvrir Wroclaw à pied. Très bon petit déjeuner. Possibilité de parking privé (payant).
neg_text: Etablissement et décors vieillots. Hôtel situé dans une impasse qui accueille aussi plusieurs cafés et donc plusieurs terrasses. Notre chambre donnait sur cette impasse et nous avons passé deux nuits épouvantables : impossible de dormir à cause des voix, des rires et des cris provenant des terrasses situées juste en-dessous. Tapage, donc,, jusque tard dans la nuit, même en pleine semaine. Pas de climatisation dans les chambres.

title: Uwaga! Hotel nie wystawia faktury.
neg_text:
Uwaga!!!
1. Hotel pobrał opłatę z karty prywatnej bez pytania, zanim pojawiłem się w hotelu i to pomimo zaznaczenia opcji płatności przy zameldowaniu. Chciałem zapłacić kartą firmową i wziąć fakturę.
2. Po pobraniu płatności hotel bez pytania o fakturę wystawia paragon przez co nie jest możliwe wystawienie faktury na firmę.

title: I advise against it.
pos_text: Photos and description of the facility on the internet.
neg_text: The standard of the facility is like that of a poor holiday resort. A tight room with sloping ceilings, which means the actual usable area of the room is smaller. A very uncomfortable bed, the frame is a wooden board. The sheet is too short, shorter than the mattress. Falling out sockets. Low water pressure in the bathroom. It is worth knowing that the windows overlook the courtyard, where people party until late at night. Closing the windows does not help you sleep. I strongly advise against this property if your intention is to stay overnight. If this were a museum and not a four-star hotel, I would give it a 7/10 because the atmosphere of the place might be appealing, but the facility is listed as a four-star hotel, which misleads customers.

title: Very poor
pos_text: Location, decent breakfast.
neg_text: If someone likes traveling to the past, they will be satisfied. The furnishings have probably not changed since the 1990s, the rooms are dark, the sockets are torn from the wall, the hooks in the bathroom are broken. The bed is uncomfortable. Not very friendly service at the reception. The elevator still remembers food stamps. Overall, dark and dingy, the hotel is screaming for renovation. I do not recommend it.

title: Very poor
pos_text: Dining room
neg_text: The room is dark, the windows are from another era, the furniture is painted, the paint has worn off after washing, in some places it's ugly and gloomy, like in a scary mansion, the windows can't be opened because downstairs there are guests sitting and talking until 2 a.m. and the echo carries, tuned vehicles drive down the street, you can't go to sleep, it's just a scary mansion, only there are no ghosts.

title: Never again!
pos_text: location - and that's it
neg_text: Old facility. You can style something as an "old object", but you have to do it tastefully. in the case of this hotel, it's simply old worn-out furniture, carpets, etc. I felt like I was in a convent with some nuns. HORRIBLE noises that prevented sleep (from the yard due to a party in the outdoor catering area, and from the street due to car races, fights and noise from the Market Square). Repulsive object! I definitely DO NOT recommend it!!!

title: Wrocław is a beautiful city, but staying in this hotel spoiled the good impression.
pos_text: Breakfast is one big disaster! We ate three breakfasts, so we have a comparison. Every time it was the same and what was supposed to be warm - scrambled eggs and sausages - was cold. I don't even want to mention it.
neg_text: I didn't like practically everything. From the deception that the hotel has parking, when in fact it does not have any parking, through a room with damaged windows, to a hopeless breakfast and unpleasant service.

title: 4 stars???!!!! How is that possible?
pos_text: Location, right next to the market square
neg_text: I still wonder how the hotel was awarded 4 stars??!!!! Doesn't even deserve 1... The hotel is old, neglected and left to itself. Something like this - people are ok, there is no need to change, modernize or repair anything... I will definitely not choose this hotel for my next stay in the beautiful city of Wrocław. Ps. The best is the lady at breakfast who attacks the guests (while the guests are scooping up food) which room are they from. Can you really not organize such a simple thing in a different, more cultured and friendly way???

title: Bad
pos_text: Thread
neg_text: No air conditioning, noise

title: If you want to get any sleep at all DO NOT STAY at this hotel!
pos_text: Pleasant staff, great location, good breakfast
neg_text:
Extremely loud outside! Did not get any sleep the 2 nights we stayed there. Extremely loud cars and motorcycles racing all night long starting at 7 pm (1900) I swear they were going back and forth on the street in front of us all night and along with that the people in the bar near us were extremely loud all night long. There is no air in the room, we had one window open to get air, we closed it because of all this of the noise, it didnt quiet the noise and our room got extremely hot and stuffy. The noise did not stop until 7 am. The pillows were ridiculous, one pillow per person and they were as flat as a pancake when you laid your head on them.
The elevator is old, there’s a big dent in the doors, sometimes it would not open or close completely., we had to help it!

title: Very poor
pos_text: Nothing
neg_text: Extremely loud, staff unhelpful, and breakfast terrible.

title: Very poor
neg_text: Unfortunately, I didn't sleep a wink, there was a disco in front of the hotel, the young people didn't let me sleep until 6 a.m. All you could hear were screams, squeals, etc. tragedy after the night my wife and I were exhausted.

title: Bad
pos_text: Overall the hotel is ok.
neg_text: Information for booking customers. Do not provide your main credit card details on booking. Booking no longer has sufficient security, hackers steal card details and then funds from the account. This happened to me when I booked at this hotel. Despite my contact, neither booking nor the hotel felt obliged to provide compensation or a refund. I had to pay for the entire reservation, plus the money that was stolen from me via booking.com. Only provide details of cards that have nothing on them or are blocked. This is how you have to deal with this incompetence of booking. The hotel did not even contact booking.com regarding this matter, despite my request. We cannot afford such situations.

title: The worst hotel
neg_text: Absolutely nothing matched the offer. Contrary to the offer, there is no parking, and according to the staff, a 7-year-old child has to sleep in a double bed. Towels with holes in the bathroom. Generally, conditions are worse than in a mountain hut.

title: The best time was, when we LEFT the hotel.
neg_text: One should not stay in this hotel. In front of the windows is an alley with 3 different restaurant . The noise was unbelievable the entire night, till 2 o clock a.m. the room was not cleaned. You can not sleep in this hotel .

title: This hotel could be much better, with very little effort. Tea bags, coffee and milk in the bedrooms (and a spoon). Get a
pos_text: Excellent location, clean rooms, carpets looked very tired and worn.
neg_text:
Pillows on the bed were useless way too soft, had to use a folded up dressing gown for support. There’s a kettle in the room, but no tea, coffee, milk, sugar or even a spoon.
Breakfast can be chaotic, not enough capacity for the number of guests. Plenty of selection of pastries, cold meats, cheeses, juices, etc. Toaster like most hotels, completely useless , and only four slots when there are about 50+ guests. Most just gave up.
Bacon usually cold unless you got a fresh batch, same with scrambled eggs. They cooked me two fresh fried eggs which was a massive improvement.

title: The hotel's location in the historic center, which will make up for the hotel's old age
pos_text:
The hotel's location in relation to the historic center is absolutely luxurious... Parking for 50 PLN/day nearby, but we didn't use it.
Breakfast was great, rich, pleasant and helpful staff.
Filtered water available on the floor in unlimited quantities.
neg_text:
The hotel is long past its best... it doesn't even reach 4* by mistake.
The furniture is old, creaky, the bathroom is dirty. The bathtub was quite slippery when you wanted to use it as a shower. Dust on the bedside tables... Paper walls - we could hear almost everything from the neighbors the first night.
Wellness - cleanliness again not so great. Rather colder water, but that's about everyone's preferences. I can't rate the fitness center, it was closed due to renovation.

title: Due to its central location, it is suitable for a short stay.
pos_text:
The hotel's location is very good; you can reach the city center in just a few minutes. The room was very quiet. The room was of sufficient size. The breakfast selection was adequate.
The head chef was very nice and creative, and the evening menu tasted very good.
neg_text:
The furnishings were in need of renovation; the shower head could not be locked in place and was far too heavy.
The menu selection in the evening was very limited, especially since we had expected a buffet.
The drink prices were clearly too high (beer 0.5 l for €6.70)

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Below std for the price

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Damaged room furnishings: bathroom door, poor condition of bathroom infrastructure

title: Perfect location, good breakfast, nice staff in a slightly outdated hotel.
pos_text:
Location: short walking distance to the city's most important sights, restaurants, cafes, etc.
The staff was very helpful and nice.
The breakfast was varied and good.
There was a coffee machine with free coffee almost all day.
neg_text:
The fitness room was known to be unavailable.
The pool was much smaller in reality than it looked in the picture, so nice if you want to relax in the water, but it's too small for swimming.
The room was a bit old.

title: It is necessary to add sun loungers or at least chairs by the pool, control the water temperature and the operation of the sauna.
pos_text: Excellent breakfasts for a 4* hotel and location.
neg_text:
I booked this hotel solely for the pool, gym and sauna.
On the day of arrival, they initially refused to give me a robe, citing the fact that the hotel was overbooked and all the robes had already been taken for today, so come back tomorrow. then they said that I had a cheap room, since it was for one person. ( I don't understand anything here at all, is it cheaper because it's for one person) or don't all room categories include a robe in a hotel with a pool?
An hour later, another extremely nice young man, without any questions, arranged for two robes to be delivered to my room in 15 minutes.
But, as it turned out, that day the pool had very cold water, and the sauna was practically not heating. The gym was completely closed. There were 3 sun loungers, which is very few. On the second day, the pool became a little warmer, but still uncomfortable, and the sauna was even colder. One sun lounger was already broken and there were 2 left. Then I just got sick, I assume it was because of the hypothermia in their “spa” and for the third day I didn’t go anywhere, and there was no point.
Also, on the second day, there were no towels in the locker room at this spa and there was no one at the spa reception desk to ask for anything.
If you don't need all that, it's a normal average hotel in the city center with good breakfasts.

title: I do not recommend it due to renovation
pos_text: Close to the center, small swimming pool and sauna
neg_text: Renovation in the text of the stay. The offer was non-refundable, and we received information about the renovation the day before arrival, even though it was known about a week earlier at the time of booking. Zero information about renovation (dirt, drilling, workers in the rooms next door)

title: Passable
neg_text: One key for the entire wardrobe. No glasses in the room. At the request of the receptionist I heard" they will be when I find them." Great that it worked out. I don't think anyone has ever cleaned under the bed. The day before we spent at Qubus Kraków was much better. Apart from the location and delicious breakfast, I do not recommend it. Drama room. No one informed us about the renovation on the first floor and the work that had been going on since the morning. The gym is located at the swimming pool. Literally.

title: I expected more from Qubus 4*..
pos_text: The location is definitely a plus, very close to everything. The room was clean, tidy, warm and quiet.
neg_text: The food, from the restaurant to the breakfast which was included in the price - was a disaster. A 4-star hotel, we assumed that the food would be first class. For dinner we ordered (on our own) ramen with duck (the duck was brown, it should have been pink, the broth tasted like water with a few drops of magic, raw carrots and mushrooms. I specifically called the waitress to witness that the carrot I bit into was crunchy because it was raw. A dish for PLN 49, completely tasteless. The żurek soup tasted and smelled like mushroom soup, and the waitress confirmed that she could smell the mushrooms :) Breakfast? - dried hams, hard cheeses, scrambled eggs made from powder (100%). Even the tea had no taste (unknown brand, probably the cheapest). Overall, the kitchen is a disaster. The ladies at the reception were unfortunately not from Poland, many things were not understandable to them… Moreover, the swimming pool and jacuzzi are closed, which we only find out after paying for the reservation, which is very poor.

title: Close to the center
pos_text: Great location and delicious breakfast
neg_text: Very old

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Hotel location. In the immediate vicinity of the Old Town. Very tasty and rich breakfasts. Moreover, we used the half-board option and it was a really great solution. The dishes served at the hotel reservation deserve the highest rating...large portions, beautifully presented and very tasty. Theoretically, the size of the room is a plus, but you have to take into account that I consciously chose a larger but also more expensive room.
neg_text: Well...the fitness center and spa are probably a joke. On the so-called The gym had one exercise bike - non-functional, although supposedly new, one elliptical trainer - also non-functional, one treadmill, which, to put it mildly, could be described as worn out, but its cleanliness was a disaster... dirty, sticky, the phone holder literally melted... below criticism. There was also a strength training atlas, which also dates back to very distant times. Additionally, the above equipment is set up in one room with a swimming pool, jacuzzi and sauna... so it is hot, humid and not very pleasant/comfortable.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location in the city center, large, spacious room and bathroom, comfortable sauna, tasty and varied breakfasts.
neg_text: The cleanliness of the pool was poor, one remote control for the garage door limited me, the staff did not clean the room upon my request.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Okay
neg_text: No parking

title: Disappointing
pos_text: It’s centrally located.
neg_text: The hotel is quite old and needs a renovation. There was a strong smell of cigarettes in the elevator. There was a repair in the corridor, the whole corridor looked like a construction site- they could at least inform about it during check in. The pool was way too cold to swim. And there was some disgusting foam coming out of jacuzzi, with some trash lying around.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The short connection to the beautiful old town

title: Opinion
pos_text: The hotel has a very good location. And that's all about the advantages of this hotel.
neg_text: The hotel stopped in the times of the Polish People's Republic. Everything is old, the furniture, the bathroom. Requiring renovation. The hotel offers a swimming pool and a sauna, but there are no bathrobes in the hotel and no hotel slippers in the rooms. If you want to go down to the pool, you must do so in normal clothes and change at the pool. For me it's mega weak. Price to quality ratio - excessive price. Totally not worth the price. For a one-night stay for a mother and child, without meals, I paid almost PLN 600. The hotel is not worth the price.

title: Hotel for one night.
pos_text: The hotel is very well located. Its splendor is already beyond itself. Breakfasts are modest. Parking is remote and paid.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The location is great.
Breakfast average.
neg_text: The hotel clearly needs an upgrade to meet the current 4* standard

title: I'm never going there again for that price.

neg_text:
The staff is simply disorganized and not exactly friendly.
I had to wait 45 minutes and call twice just for slippers. Then I simply went down myself and got them, and also told you that you could have told me during the first phone call to come down and get them myself, because otherwise it takes 3 days.

title: Passable
pos_text: Breakfast
neg_text: Dirty pool, grease all around. Mold in the shower by the pool. Missing towels. Lack of cleanliness even in the room.

title: Passable
pos_text: Great location, great staff and great food too
neg_text:
For such a low price in the market itself it was great
The only thing that needed replacing was the door, it was scratched, the pool was cold, the sauna was too hot, the elevators were old, I was too embarrassed to get into the bathtub, I felt a bit like I was at my grandma's in the countryside

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Lokalizacja.

title: Umiarkowanie zadowalająco
neg_text: Zakamieniony prysznic z którego nie leciała woda , pęknięte fugi w płytkach wyglądające jak grzyb , brak szlafroków bo pralnia nie przyjechała , śniadania słabe bardzo , w dniu przyjazdu restauracja czynna od 17 miałem ochotę coś zjeść a tu nie było jak

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Hotel jest w rewelacyjnym miejscu, niemalże centrum starego rynku.
neg_text: Mało zróżnicowane śniadania. Co dzień to samo.

title: Great location but still stuck in the 1980’s
pos_text: Location is great
neg_text: It’s a little tired

title: It's clean and has a pool.
pos_text: Good location, swimming pool excellent.
neg_text: Only one pillow on each bed and towels were less than one would expect (1very small guest towel+1 bath towel pp+1 bath mat per room) telephone in room didn't work so one of us had to get dressed to go and ask for more. Reported telephone twice but no action taken during 3 night stay.

pos_text:
pokój 508, Bardzo dobre śniadanie , różnorodne, nie brakowało niczego, obsługa miła, pomocna szybko reaguje na braki.
Świetny pomysł z dystrybutorem wody na korytarzu , wystrój pokoju bardzo ładny, dwie łazienki
neg_text:

title: NOT MY FAVORITE
pos_text: Delicious and rich breakfast. During my 3 nights I wasn't able to try everything. Very good coffee. Tasty food in the restaurant. Friendly reception staff and very professional in the restaurant. Hotel in the center of the Old Town - close to everything. Swimming pool and jacuzzi on site and possibility of using spa treatments. Night security guard (older man) very empathetic, polite and helpful. Water in dispensers on each floor instead of bottles - a very nice and eco-friendly idea. Comfortable beds. The room has a fridge and a balcony. Low price for the standard and location.
neg_text: A 100 PLN voucher to be used in the hotel restaurant does not compensate for the mess and dirt that was in the room. Night lamps - layer of dust. Contacts - last washed half a year ago. The bathroom is full of hair from the previous tenants. Pubic hair in the bidet. YUCK!!! Pieces of glass in the bedsheets. After reporting the problem - it was not fixed. The bed linen was not changed. The hair remained. After another intervention - the bathroom is so-so. The bedding is still the same and it wasn't even laid out as part of the service. These are not 4-star hotel standards. The water in the pool, which is supposed to be recreational, is freezing cold. It's definitely not 27 degrees as it says on the board. The jacuzzi is dirty - you can see it by the color of the foam. As part of the training, I suggest sending employees to Qubus in Krakow or Gdansk. There, 4 stars mean a 4-star standard, not a low-quality motel like in Wrocław. Never again. I do not recommend it.

neg_text:
Firstly, the aesthetics of the corridors - the carpet, probably burned by chemicals, looked like it was from a cheap hostel. Horror.
Secondly - an elevator with scratched handrails and worn-out instructions according to which one must use the key card before choosing the floor; no markings on which floor the elevator stops.
Thirdly, information - after booking, when calling and asking about parking options - I was informed that the parking lot was 350 m away and cost PLN 120, so I organised parking in a different way. When checking in, it turned out that there was another parking lot available for PLN 40 (I did not receive this information when I asked during the phone call).
Fourth - cleaning products in the bathroom. I have no complaints about the general cleanliness, but the products used in the bathroom caused shoes to stick uncomfortably to the floor.
Fifthly - a nice lighting effect in the bathroom - the wall was made of glass doors leading to a balcony shared by several rooms, but .... matte foil on one of the windows was carelessly glued, which meant that when standing on the balcony in the evening, with the bathroom light on - the person standing on the balcony (I remind you of the shared balcony) has an excellent view of the person using the bathroom and unless you are an exhibitionist, this is very uncomfortable.
I won't add that when asked at the pool whether there are towels available, the answer is "if they're not hanging there, then there aren't any."

title: If someone likes sleeping in an ice hotel, this will be a good choice.
pos_text: Location. Breakfast ok.
neg_text: Well, yes, I chose the hotel based on repeated good experiences at the Qubus hotel in Krakow, which meets all 4* standards and is simply much better in every respect than the facility in Wrocław, in my opinion. The facility in Wrocław offers check-in from 3 p.m. What a surprise we had when we arrived at the hotel at 3:20 p.m. We saw the reception desk, hmm, honestly, in motels it is more representative than in the Qubus Hotel Wrocław, we proceed to check-in, unfortunately the room it turns out that the reception was not ready to offer us any coffee or anything while we were waiting for the room that was supposed to be waiting for us... okay, after a few minutes the room is ready... We dropped our luggage in the room and went into town to take care of things. After returning home, the room was cold, 18 degrees Celsius, no, it wasn't because of the open windows or something, it was just 18 degrees Celsius in the room... We set the airflow to 23 degrees and unfortunately, under the duvet, the airflow was terribly cold... so I had to turn it off, hide under the covers, put on a hat and try to sleep. In the morning, the temperature in the room was around 16 degrees. I didn't test the pool, even though this was one of the factors that determined my choice of hotel, but if it was also 16 degrees there, I preferred to skip it... Breakfast was okay, although compared to Qubus Kraków, it was poor... When we told the reception that the room temperature was 16 degrees, we were told that the heating season had not started yet (the temperature was 2 degrees at night). Well, generally speaking, just as I have always recommended the facility in Krakow, I will definitely advise against the facility in Wrocław because the location does not compensate for the rest, especially considering the price of less than PLN 700 per night.

Niestety mój pobyt w tym hotelu był dużym rozczarowaniem. Obsługa sprawia wrażenie niestety mało kompetentnej. Zgłosiłam problem z nieczystością w pokoju, jednak nie spotkało się to z żadną reakcją ani zainteresowaniem ze strony personelu.

Czystość pokoju pozostawia wiele do życzenia. Kiedy przypadkowo upuściłam pomadkę pod łóżko i zajrzałam pod nie, znalazłam tam nie tylko kurz, ale nawet kartę do apartamentu po poprzednich gościach. To pokazuje, że sprzątanie nie jest wykonywane dokładnie.

Problem pojawił się również przy próbie skorzystania z ekspresu, sytuacja była bardzo nieprzyjemna, gdy chciałam usiąść z kawą w holu zamiast iść z nią do pokoju. Pani z obsługi przewróciła na mnie oczami, co było bardzo nieprofesjonalne i sprawiło, że poczułam się niekomfortowo.

Kolejnym minusem jest kwestia parkingu. W udogodnieniach hotelu widnieje informacja o „prywatnym parkingu”, co może sugerować, że znajduje się on przy hotelu. Dopiero po dokonaniu rezerwacji doczytałam, że parking znajduje się około 500 metrów od obiektu i kosztuje aż 200 zł za weekend.

Niestety przez te wszystkie sytuacje mój pobyt nie był udany i trudno mi polecić to miejsce.

pos_text: Great location in the old town, just a few steps from the Advent markets. The view from the window is straight to the cathedral, the room is clean - it matches the photos. neg_text: Very small and dirty wellness area (small pool, even smaller jacuzzi and sauna for 2 people. The floor in the wellness area was very dirty and when the hot tub was turned on, mountains of foam rolled down (a sign of dirty water). At the same time, the advertised parking is located in an independent parking garage within 10 minutes of the hotel. Getting a parking space here during the weekend (and especially during Advent) took about 30 minutes of driving up and down floors and desperately searching.

title: Poor

title: I think it's time for a change...

The room had a bit of a strange smell, but overall it was clean. I think the rooms could use a refresh as they've seen their share of life :-)
neg_text: In the SPA zone (swimming pool, jacuzzi and sauna) German tourists (middle-aged :-)) They walked around naked, which made the aesthetic impression of the rest of the users not the best. Unfortunately, the staff was unresponsive and several people with children had to cancel their stay. Everyone pays the same, so it's hard to understand the attitude of the hotel and the staff.The hotel booking service only offers breakfast options on weekends, which means there are crowds in the morning and a queue in the breakfast room, and instead of peacefully enjoying the delicious food, those eating feel the eyes of people in the queue who also want to eat. This is the first time we encountered something like this. The hotel is in the center of Wrocław and there are plenty of restaurants serving breakfast around - which would solve the problem, but no - the option without breakfast on the weekend - is not available.

title: If someone only cares about the location, I recommend it, breakfast, rooms, no air conditioning at night, rather poor - I wouldn't recommend the hotel
pos_text: Location, right in the center
neg_text: In the first room the refrigerator and air conditioning did not work. The man who came to turn it on made some attempts to turn it on but was unsuccessful and told us to wait until it worked and call if anything happened. We called and asked for a room change. Another mega ugly room a'la Louis XVI. The fridge works, and the air conditioning seems to work too, but the hotel turns off the air conditioning at night, which was not mentioned when booking. We were there on August 19-20, the heat was terrible, even at night it was 23 degrees, so the lack of air conditioning was a disaster. Breakfast was rather poor for PLN 75 per person. I think that for that kind of money per person you can easily eat delicious food at the market.

title: We won't be back

neg_text:
Our room facing the backyard 5th floor. The backyard is full of parking spaces, air conditioning units, and other technical equipment, making it very noisy. It was not possible to open the windows at night, with daytime and room temperature at 32 degrees. The backyard is beautifully sunny during the day. The air conditioning, like the room, was a bit dated. If the temperature is set to 16 degrees and the room is cooled down automatically... forget it.
No cleaning of the rooms, only changing of towels and making of beds. They looked like someone had just slept in them 🤔. We would not choose this hotel for another stay in Wroclaw. Here, higher price and services do not match.

title: Paid full price to stay in a building site!!!!!
pos_text: Location is excellent, 100 yes from main square.
neg_text:
Renovation work being carried out, we were only notified of this 24 hours before departure & were given a room which was on the same floor as the work being done, first two days were OK but third day we were woken up at 9am by a very loud drilling noise which was unbearable, we phoned to complain to see if they could get it stopped but we were told no, noise continued. We phoned again & they offered to move rooms, we accepted but couldn't understand why they didn't give us that room in the first place away from the work area, when we asked we were told they had lots of rooms away from the work. Not quite the relaxing few days away we were expecting.
The room we were given was much better but not 100% finished, there was bare concrete/ reinforcing showing in the ceiling & capped off pipes for a radiator sticking through the wall, not ideal.
Staff weren't particularly helpful, customer service goes a long way & they would do well to remember that, we were given two complimentary drink vouchers as an apology but when we used them we had to pay 1zl so they weren't complimentary, not a lot of money I know but considering they were given as an apology you'd think they'd have waved it.
The whole thing just made me even more annoyed because it could have been so much better with a little forethought.
Don't stay here until the renovations are complete.

title: Odradzamy z całego serca.
pos_text: Śniadanie w porządku. To jedyna zaleta tego hotelu.
neg_text:
Fatalny hotel pod wieloma względami.
Po pierwsze, po intensywnym dniu zwiedzania Wrocławia, nie mogliśmy z żoną wziąć prysznica przed snem. Lodowata woda... Zgłoszone na recepcję ok. godz. 22. Najpierw złota rada, żebym przekręcił kurek tam gdzie czerwona kropka i poczekał dłużej. Nie skorzystałem i upierałem się, że wody ciepłej nie ma. Usłyszałem, że za chwilę ktoś przyjdzie sprawdzić. Oczywiście nikt nie przyszedł. Kolejny telefon wykonałem po 15 minutach i dowiedziałem się, że jest awaria tylko w moim pokoju i już jest naprawiane. Po 20 minutach zadzwoniłem po raz kolejny i usłyszałem, że już naprawione, woda ciepła jest. Sprawdzam - oczywiście nie ma. Dzwonię. "Woda będzie za 20-25 minut jak tylko boiler się nagrzeje". Tego już było za wiele. Oczywiście wiem, że woda w wielkim zapewne bojlerze nie nagrzeje się w 20 minut. Proszę o inny pokój. Ok, pan już sprawdza. Dzwoni po kilku minutach i mówi, że w całym hotelu są jeszcze dwa wolne pokoje, ale -uwaga- w nich jest ten sam problem z wodą, jak twierdzi. Już nie tylko w naszym pokoju. Zapachniało "ściemą". Koło 23 poszliśmy spać brudni.
To główny zarzut. O drobiazgach jak niedziałająca jedna z dwóch wind w hotelu i konieczności chodzenia po schodach na 3 piętro nie wspomnę. Cały hotel jeżdżący jedną windą spowodował, że bardzo długo trzeba było na nią czekać. Strata czasu.
Poza tym odrapane stare meble i drzwi w pokojach. Ale to są oczywiście drobiazgi.
A, jeszcze ściany w pokoju wybitnie nie dźwiękoszczelne. Sąsiadów z pokoju obok słychać jakby stali obok w naszym pokoju, a chrapiący sąsiad jakby spał z nami w łóżku.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
We had the misfortune of sleeping in two rooms. The first one had an uncomfortable mattress, the room was rather classic, nothing fancy, but also without any major flaws. There was nothing more than we expected from a hotel. However, on the first day our toilet flush did not work. We reported it and received a response that someone would come to us. We were there for work, so we were out of the room all day. After returning, (around midnight) we saw that the flush still didn't work. So we called the reception desk, and the gentleman there was completely pissed off at us, he told us he was very sorry, but there was nothing he could do because it was Friday evening and the technician didn't work on weekends, and he would suggest changing the room. However, the next morning we would have to go back to the old room with a broken flush (so the room change would only be for the night). As compensation, he offered us breakfast, but we had already purchased it, and free parking for one day (we didn't come by car). We decided to go down to the reception to talk to the gentleman again. The gentleman at the reception desk from Friday to Saturday showed such a lack of professionalism that we were completely taken aback. I am leaving aside the basic rules of communication, such as: It would be better to stand up instead of sitting with your legs spread apart, as you would when talking to another person, but he still stuck to what he had said earlier on the phone. We refused to change rooms for the night, because we least need the toilet at night and it would cause too much confusion.
The next morning, we noticed another gentleman at the reception desk, very nice and polite (actually, the entire staff, apart from one gentleman on night duty from Friday to Saturday, was very nice and polite, helpful, and even supportive, which was a pleasant surprise). Ultimately, it turned out that it was possible to exchange the room, with a working flush.

title: I recommend it, but there are some drawbacks
pos_text:
The best advantage of the hotel is breakfast. The price for breakfast is quite high, but worth it all. The breakfasts were very good, fresh and there was everything you could want: granola, various types of yogurt, muesli, milk, fresh fruit, juicer, fruit juices, scrambled eggs, eggs, bacon, baked vegetables, omelets, cheese, blue and blue cheeses, olives, various salads, cold cuts, butter, coffee, tea. Vegetarian zone: vegan cheese, cold cuts, cheese, plant-based sausages, plant-based milk. Chia, tapioca. For coffees, teas, ginger, lemon, white sugar, brown sugar, sweeteners. Chocolate cream, maple syrup. Still, sparkling, cold water. Really… there is everything. Additionally, there is the option of breakfast boxes, which are really very filling. There are also highchairs for children in the breakfast restaurant, so that's very nice. Reception. This is also a huge plus. Nice people and in fact, they solve every problem and whim. We wanted a non-slip mat, we got it. We wanted a candle, we got it. And other things too, which brings us to another advantage… Housekeeping. The maids are wonderful and do a good job. We asked that all glass items and decorations be removed from the room before arrival, as we have small children and we did not want them damaged. And everything was hidden and secured. When cleaning was ordered, it was obvious that it was clean and quick. Women really do make it. Eco-friendly hotel. There are waste sorting bins in the rooms. They do not clean rooms every day, only upon request. At breakfast they make smoothies from the remaining fruit. All products are eco and healthy. Additionally, you can take coffee to go with breakfast. They offer paper cups, which is a huge plus, because you usually can't take anything out of the kitchen. There is a small “shop” in the lobby where you can buy everything you need. Tampons, pads, razor, shampoo, body lotion, and snacks + coffee machine. Prices are high, but things are needed.
The Stockholm restaurant, although open at low hours, has nice service. Delicious drinks. Soundproof rooms.
neg_text:
-There was a huge luggage trolley at the entrance to the hotel, but no one offered to take our luggage, even though we had a lot of luggage.
-At the very bottom there is the Stockholm restaurant, which is unfortunately open from Tuesday to Saturday and from 5 p.m. This is very problematic because breakfast is until 10:00, so I have to wait until 17:00 for dinner.
-Fitness room (gym and sauna) is located on the first floor, next to the conference rooms. To get there you have to go through the corridor through all the conference rooms. Which may be uncomfortable when you want to go, for example, to the gym already dressed.
Hotel Room (standard family room)
-The bathroom is missing a single light. When you want to use the toilet. e.g. at night, all the big lights come on at once.
-The bathroom lacks a towel hook, e.g. to your hands so that it is at hand.
-The bathroom has a bathtub with the option of a shower. There is a glass pane that is placed in an unfortunate place because you cannot turn on the water through the glass.
-The basket in the bathroom is very small.
-There is a bin in the room for sorting, but it is very small. With 4 people in the room, we had to ask for extra garbage disposal every day because we didn't fit.
-We had one large bed and one sofa bed. This sofa was a disaster. It's scary for an adult to sit there, let alone sleep, because every time they turn around, the whole thing moves and squeaks. It's impossible to sleep.
-A 4* hotel should have room service as standard, but unfortunately it doesn't. What a pity.
-The fridge is very small and not normal, it only keeps the temperature a little.
-There is no WI-FI on the TV, you can't start e.g. Netflix. Just regular television.
-The carpet is so colored that it appears very dirty.
-The air conditioning worked as it should and was very loud. Additionally, it was written that the air conditioning would turn off automatically when the window was opened - this did not happen.

Something urgently needs to change here. It is unacceptable that the guests' sleep is disturbed by a fan running continuously. title: Long wait at check-in
pos_text: Comfortable room, excellent breakfast
neg_text:
1. I arrived at the hotel at 12 noon on the day of arrival. I was asked at reception to wait until 3 pm. Only then would check-in be possible and the room ready for occupancy.
Okay, I came back at 2 pm after a walk through the city. Once again, I was told to come back at 3 pm.

Is it really too much to ask that rooms be prepared a little earlier and not be rigidly insisted upon by the fixed time?

So I had to wait another hour in the lobby until the room was finally ready. Then only after repeated requests at reception. The journey had been tiring, and all they wanted to do was relax a bit in their room.

It might only take 20 minutes to prepare a single room.

The organization is poor in this regard.
I will arrive after 3 pm on my next visit.

The Scandic is not some fly-by-night hotel, but a large, reputable hotel.

2. When I checked out, the young man at reception wanted to charge me separately for breakfast, even though it was already included in the package and I had already paid. The error has been resolved. The employee did not apologize, which I had hoped for.

Overall, I had the impression that Polish guests were treated more kindly and courteously than Germans.

Lack of room cleaning during the stay is acceptable in a two- or three-star hotel, but the Scandic hotel has four of them. It seems that these stars should be verified. When you touch anything in the hotel, you feel like your hands are covered in all kinds of bacteria and viruses.
The underground car park is not secured, which is indicated not by the staff but by the inscription on the parking ticket.
Too small a restaurant for the number of hotel rooms. To eat breakfast during a long weekend, you have to wait in line for a free table. And once you're inside, you discover there are no plates, cups, knives or spoons. The staff tried to make up for it with a good face and an apologetic smile, but such things have no right to happen in a four-star hotel.

Hello dear Karolina,

Unfortunately I am forced to write this complaint letter. Reasons is that I am supposed to stay 9 night in Wroclaw, so it was important for me to find a good hotel with possibility to train during my stay. That is the reason why the choice was Scandic Wroclaw , since it is 4 star hotel and you had training facility in the hotel. When I arrive and went down to the spinning room. I found the machine threadmill broken, Strength bench unstable, water refreshment machine not functioning. I have from day 1 in the beginning raise this to the receptionist, they said they will take care of it. Unfortunately still the issues maintain as unsolved. I Would appreciate some compensation, due to that the expectations regarding what the Hotel should offer is not met.

Please advise.

Br
Edwin Espinoza

title: Last time ever staying in Scandic
neg_text:
I have been staying at Scandic Wroclaw for around 9 years now, visiting this hotel at least once a year during my visits in Wroclaw, but this will be the last time I have stayed at this hotel.

The quality of services and facilities has been on a downward trend for a while but this time topped it all.

I have been in put in a room next to a room(s?) that had renovation works happening in it, which included heavy drilling, moving furniture and hammering the walls. There was no information on the website or booking about any renovation works happening (reception apologised for it) and I have no idea which manager thought it was ok to put guests next to rooms where heavy works is happening since early morning hours. This has been a leisure visit and I don’t need drills in the wall waking me up at 9am during my Christmas (!) stay in Wroclaw.

I wish I could upload a video here. It was so noisy I couldn’t hear the TV with the sound all way up.

The only saving grace here was reception offering me a different room and an upgrade but the management of this hotel just lost a loyal customer.

It's hard to objectively call the hotel a four-star hotel - maybe it once met the requirements, today it's an average three-star hotel. The decor in the photos looks great, but in reality the hotel is worn, torn and cracked. As a host, I would be ashamed to provide guests with a room where the shower didn't work - it was impossible to set the shower handle at the right level above the head because it kept sliding down - the mechanism is probably x years old. The door to the room had a large gap between the frame, which intensified the audibility of sounds from the corridor. In the room we found a used capsule in the coffee machine, dirty water with limescale in the kettle, and dirty spoons, which effectively discouraged us from using this equipment. No iron in the room. The hair dryer is a relic of old swimming pools, it only dries short hair (men's), drying women's hair would probably take 3 days. The air conditioning was unbearably loud, it would have been impossible to sleep with it on - we decided to open the windows instead, which would probably have been impossible on the weekend due to the noise from the courtyard (the restaurant below the windows). The elevator smelled like carpet that had never been washed.
The hotel is simply begging for renovation.

title: Horrible
pos_text: Central location
neg_text:
After reading the reviews, we decided to stay at this hotel, but unfortunately, it was a real disappointment. In our humble opinion, this property does not have the characteristics of a 4-star hotel; to be generous, I would only give it 3 stars. The furniture and bathrooms are very dated and the rooms are small. The reception staff was very rude both during check in and also when, the next day, we complained because at 4pm. 00 the rooms had not yet been cleaned. The girl at reception arrogantly said that they should prioritise cleaning the rooms needed for tourists checking in, without even apologising for the inconvenience. So if you want to take a shower or rest for an hour in the afternoon, it's better to change hotels. Cleaning only consisted of changing towels and making the bed in a cursory manner. We found the hair we left on the bathroom floor in the same place. The rooms were equipped with a coffee maker and a kettle. It's a shame that we only found the pods on the first day, from the second day onwards nothing could be done, the machines were only there for show. Breakfast left a lot to be desired. Not much selection in the sweets department. Every day only croissants and a small portion of cake. Only two types of jam and in small quantities, not enough for everyone. Nutella or other non-existent chocolate spread. Every day the same variety of savory dishes and bread, not always fresh that day. In short, a big disappointment, nothing to do with the
other 4-star hotels where we stayed in other cities in Poland.

title: They turned the hotel into a disco without warning.
pos_text: The accommodation is very central and the rooms are comfortable and clean
neg_text:
A party was held at the hotel, but it must have been for a relative of the owners because it got out of hand and the staff did nothing.
It was being held on the ground floor, but at midnight they started playing disco music at full volume, and I say that literally because the wall of my room and the mattress were vibrating.
I went down to reception at 00:30 and could see that the doors and windows of the bar were open so that the sound was coming out through the stairs into the rooms.
There were people not only on the ground floor, but also on the first floor talking and drinking and, as I said, listening to the music which was at full volume.
When I arrived at reception and spoke to the girl, she told me that she couldn't do anything and that the party was until 3:00.
I understand that parties are held in hotels, but always trying to minimize the impact on the guest.
In this case it was the opposite, it was as if the partygoers had carte blanche and did everything possible to cause a disturbance (doors and windows open; disco music at an exaggerated volume).
If they are going to organize events of that type, they could notify people when they make the reservation, or not accept reservations on that day.
It has undoubtedly been the worst night I have ever spent in a hotel. Furthermore, I was traveling with elderly people who also couldn't sleep, not just until 3:00, but even later, due to the state of nervousness left by spending three hours feeling the sound and vibrations of the bass music.

neg_text:
We visited this hotel for Christmas, but unfortunately, our stay there ruined the holiday.

On the day of our arrival, the hotel’s kitchen stopped working at 6:00 PM, so we couldn’t order dinner.
This seemed strange, as the hotel had been constantly sending me spam emails about their Christmas menu.

There was no drinking water in the room; instead, there was an empty bottle on the table. The minibar was also empty.
When we raised this issue, the hotel staff rudely told us that we should fetch water ourselves from a cooler on the floor.
As for the empty minibar, they claimed that "this is the hotel’s policy."

The room was cold at night, even though we set the heater to maximum.
The bathroom and toilet had no heating at all, so we had to leave the door ajar to ensure a comfortable temperature by morning.

Breakfasts were mediocre and repetitive.
The carpet in the elevator was dirty, and they didn’t clean it during our entire stay.

Finally, the cherry on top: when we were checking out, the hotel attempted to charge us double for breakfasts.
So, be careful – this might be their usual practice.

neg_text:
The hotel employs too few people (or insufficiently trained) in relation to the number of guests. During breakfast, the staff was unable to clean the tables on a regular basis. Some tables were dirty for 10-15 minutes while guests waited in line for a free seat. Today around 9:30 all the bain-marie hot food dishes were empty for about 20 minutes. Plates with cold snacks (cold meats, cheeses, fruit…) also at the same time they were empty or almost empty. The staff did not replenish clean plates and cups on a regular basis. And at times they were missing. We had to ask for a refill because the staff didn't notice. It was similar with coffee machines. It was necessary to ask for more grains or milk to be added. The ladies who passed by didn't notice that anything was wrong. My request to speak to a manager was ignored because it's normal that sometimes something is missing. Yesterday at breakfast the situation looked a little better. The wait time for refilling cups, plates, and food was shorter, but still too long compared to other hotels. And it was necessary to ask the staff to replenish it, because they did not see the missing items.
The food was average.
Another downside is the very low water pressure in the shower. It takes an incredibly long time to rinse shampoo or conditioner out of your hair.
The building is showing its age and needs some refreshing.
Compared to the Radisson Blu hotels in Gdańsk and Kraków, this hotel is poor.

We didn't have any other option, so we reached out a first free hotel in the last minute to cater us, so we can can have a sleep at least for a few hours. We checked out slightly before 4.a.m. in the morning and left the hotel very dissatisfied.

On the other note, lobby bar service was super slow even though there were a few guests in the place, I waited for a good fifteen minutes at the bar to get a single drink .

Breakfast is a joke, poor selection, no live cooking and often present at lower standards. Dried out salads, cheeses or cold cuts. The service does not replenish if something is missing. neg_text: They didn't provide water in the rooms at the hotel. I arrived at the hotel around 9 PM, feeling tired and not feeling well. I asked for extra water in my room. But we were told that water service was not provided as per hotel policy. There was an empty bottle in the room, and every time she wanted water, I would go to the water source about 50 steps away and refill it. A few times... but this practice, done to avoid using plastic bottles, is accompanied by single-use coffee and tea serving dishes, and Cocktail-packaged toiletries and cleaning products in the room. It's a contradictory situation...

title: Next time I won't choose Radisson, in Wrocław or anywhere else
pos_text: Location - close to the old town
neg_text:
1. I asked to reserve a parking space - I received an answer that they cannot reserve it, but it costs PLN 110 per day.
2. After entering the room, I decided I would like a beer - I looked into the bar - empty. The lady at the reception explained that due to the pandemic, they removed drinks from the mini-bar and will probably not return them - company policy.
3. If there's no beer, I'll drink water, whatever. There is no water either, there is a carafe and a message to look for a dispenser in the corridor and pour some. Apparently they want to be ecological and have given up using plastic. They could have given it in a glass bottle. Apparently Radisson's policy too.
4. And to top it all off, there's a cloth bag on the bed with a story about how to skip cleaning the room because it's eco-friendly.
RADISSON! I have a few more ideas for you: convince your customers not to flush or wash themselves, change bed linen and towels for every third customer - you will be even more eco-friendly!!!
5. The best was at the end. When checking out, I told the lady at the reception about the above observations, to which she replied that she fully understood me. When I said I was going to share this in my hotel review, she immediately offered me a discount for my stay and asked me not to. It seems that they are afraid of bad reviews, they have a prepared response to individual cases in the form of a bribe, but they do not intend to change it systematically.
To sum up, it is not worth paying 510 for this pseudo ***** hotel. I will do that next time

Śniadania były smaczne ale bardzo mały wybór. Niektórzy zamawiali jedzenie spoza dostępnego, natomiast nie wiadomo na jakiej zasadzie, nie było o tym żadnej informacji. Overall a huge disappointment, nowhere near Radisson in other Polish cities.

How does this place have five stars?!

We had an empty shower gel bottle, a big spill on the shelf and a broken phone so could not call reception. The room itself feels worn out, like an old corporate hotel that was never updated. The fake wood has lots of scrapes and worn bits and the bathroom feels incredibly dated. Finally, like all hotels that claim to be environmentally friendly only when it saves them money, they no longer provide bottles of water, but rather a fountain you share with the whole floor.

This was an incredibly disappointing stay. The last time I stay at a Radisson for sure.

title: Beautiful hotel and facilities, but unfortunately the service and staff attitude were far below the standards expected f
pos_text: Platinum Palace Boutique Hotel & SPA is a very elegant and well-maintained hotel, with spacious rooms and a pleasant atmosphere. The location is also convenient, allowing easy access to the city center within a few minutes. The SPA is definitely a valuable addition for guests looking to relax.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, the experience was affected by several issues that I did not expect from a hotel of this standard. The staff did not seem adequately trained for a 5-star hotel and, at times, appeared not prepared to manage a luxury property. My room was not cleaned properly during the stay, it was left untidy, and I felt poorly treated when I reported the issue.

I appreciated that the management eventually agreed to a partial refund through Booking.com, but I still believe the compensation was not fully proportionate to the inconvenience experienced. Overall, the hotel has excellent potential thanks to the structure and facilities, but the level of service and guest care needs significant improvement to truly meet 5-star expectations.

title: Not a 5* hotel
pos_text: Very good breakfast and delicious food in the attached restaurant.
neg_text:
The safe was defective, and this was reported on the very first day. Nobody took care of it until we left.
There are only 2 hooks for bathrobes in the bathroom. There are no other hooks for drying towels. There is ample space available and the effort is minimal, with a great effect. The bathroom mirror stood in the window facing the main street and in the window frame. Soap dispensers and lotion dispensers were attached. Apart from the fact that it's all very impractical, you stand directly in front of the window at the washbasin. There is a roller blind intended to prevent people from seeing inside, but this then prevents them from seeing in the mirror.
There are also no hooks for hanging jackets, bags, etc. in the entire room.
Unfortunately, the room was not properly cleaned.
We tested the spa area. Here too, we must say that everything is cramped and there is no separate quiet zone.
The booked massage, however, was very good.
The staff were generally helpful, but often seemed awkward.
Overall, we have to say that the hotel does not have a 5-star level.

title: Passable
neg_text:
We stayed at this hotel for the weekend with a friend and we specifically chose it because of the relaxation area – jacuzzi and SPA, which were one of the main reasons for our booking. Unfortunately, the jacuzzi was not working for the entire two days of our stay. No one informed us about this in advance, and when we checked in they only said that "they will fix it tomorrow" – which did not happen.

The room was very cold and I would rate the cleanliness as 2/5 - definitely not at the level of a hotel that costs over PLN 600 per night. Reception service was also poor – 2/5. Upon check-out, we were not given change for 50 PLN, which I only noticed later.

The breakfasts were delicious – that's the only positive aspect of the stay.

We filed a complaint via Booking.com and received a response that the hotel could offer us… a voucher worth PLN 100 to be used for your next booking at the same facility. It is hard to consider it as taking the situation seriously – such a “gesture” after a completely inaccessible jacuzzi and numerous inconveniences it seems simply ridiculous.

We came with the intention of relaxing and unwinding in the wellness area, but we had to look for entertainment outside the hotel. If we had known that it would not be possible to enjoy a holiday of this standard, we would have definitely chosen a cheaper hotel – this one was absolutely not worth the price.

title: A 5 star that behaves like a 3 star
pos_text: Excellent breakfast, old building with modern interiors, spacious shower
neg_text: Smartv the ability to connect Apple devices or platforms such as Netflix, Disney, Prime etc. etc.; as soon as we arrived we found a bag with some food probably left by previous guests; the bathroom is too close to the bed and not very comfortable; On the last day, even though we had left the room at 10am with a card asking for it to be cleaned, the room was not cleaned and, once we pointed this out to reception, we were told that it was no longer possible and they tried to resolve the issue with a "what do you need?"... unacceptable in a 5-star hotel; little attention to detail.

title: It was good, for a while.
pos_text: A truly high-class hotel. The building itself is beautiful, the interior refined in every detail. SPA included in the price, including a mini gym where you can do cardio training. The room was spacious, clean, and prepared for our arrival. The hotel - as a building - met our expectations. The room was comfortable and beautifully situated, but unfortunately it was winter.
neg_text:
Service. Unfortunately, apart from the fantastic butler who let us into the premises, the reception (which should be impeccable in a hotel of this class) leaves much to be desired. During my stay, upon check-in, the amount for the night was charged to my card (the money was debited from my account) plus PLN 200 deposit. When checking out, the lady at the reception informed me that they were unable to confirm the pre-authorization from the card and that I had to pay for the accommodation again - and the original authorization would be returned by the bank at some point. Therefore, the hotel stay cost me PLN 3,000 instead of PLN 1,500. Now I am waiting for the money to be refunded by the bank and for the hotel to refund the PLN 200 deposit. I don't know what would have happened if I hadn't had the extra 1,500 PLN to re-download the transaction. I hope that the issue of refunding the overpayment will be resolved by the bank/hotel. This situation has ruined all the pleasure I had staying at your hotel. The ladies at the reception unfortunately showed a lack of professionalism and understanding of me as a customer.

Additionally, a higher standard room, which we chose for a given season (autumn) wasn't the most pleasant, the glass walls in the entire room and the heat vents weren't able to heat up our room - the nights were cold and we couldn't live without the vents roaring all night long - Unfortunately...

Unfortunately, our reservation coincided with the organization of a mass event and on Friday we were unable to use the restaurant upon arrival. The restaurant was also closed on Saturday... generally - if you are a hotel guest and in the meantime there is a mass event - you are lower in the hierarchy of needs than a random person from a corporate event. Pity.

Faulty shower - open (no door) the cabin causes water to splash onto the part of the bathroom outside the cabin, directly under the toilet, despite the installed threshold, and then you have to walk on water to get to the toilet, unless you waste one towel on wiping up the water. For a 5-star hotel, the breakfast could be richer. The day before I was in a 4-star hotel and the breakfast was better than the one at the Platinum. To get from the main part where the restaurant is located to the back of the hotel you have to use two elevators or an elevator and stairs - it's a bit ill-conceived.

Przy drugim telefonie ponoć w tym samym czasie miał być Panieński i pani która odebrała nie chciała mi umówić masaży, bo „niewiadomo czy ten Panieński się odbędzie”. Poprosiłam, aby państwo się ze mną skontaktowali jak będą coś już wiedzieć. Oczywiście nikt do mnie nie zadzwonił. Przy 3 telefonie z mojej strony odebrał Pan który w sumie nic nie wiedział i kazał mi się umówić później. Dzwoniąc 4!!!!!! raz Pani powiedziała, że nie ma jeszcze grafików dla masażystów. Poprosiłam o kontakt. Tak, nikt do mnie nie zadzwonił 😂

Wchodząc do recepcji przywitała mnie nieprzyjemna Pani która odniosłam wrażenie, że bardzo nie lubi swojej pracy i mnie jako człowieka. Rozmowa przy zameldowaniu była równie nieatrakcyjna jak powitanie owej Pani.

Kolejnym minusem jest to, że w godzinach 10-16 restauracja jest zamknięta i nie ma możliwości zamówić jakiegokolwiek jedzenia.

Nejvíc nás zklamalo wellness - nafoceno krásně, ale co se týče efektivity tak jedno z nejhorších,co jsme zatím byli. Fitko je tam taky jen pro hvězdičku, jinak to jsou 4 cvičící stroje na minichodbičce do wellness. Restauraci nemůžeme hodnotit, první večer byla z důvodu soukromé párty uzavřena a druhý večer jsme ji nevyužili. Celkově se tedy vau efekt nekonal a vzhledem k avizované úrovni a ceně jsme spokojeni nebyli. Ve srovnání s jinými hotely, které jsme navštívili, bychom zde dali 3 hvězdy.

title: Passable

Rovněž oceňuji hotelové parkoviště, vzhledem k menšímu počtu ubytovaných bylo míst k parkování opravdu dost.

Nutno podotknout, že mohutné krovy byly velmi nízko, takže pokud jsem si chtěla dojít k zásuvce, která byla za postelí, musela jsem se pořádně sklonit (starší osoba, příp. osoba byť jen s mírným hendikepem pohybového aparátu by byla bez šance). Jednalo se o pokoj č. 301 - dle mého názoru toto ubytování neodpovídalo standardům pokoje 5* hotelu (koupelna snad).
Snídaně byly ve velmi pěkné jídelně, co se týká nabídky jídla - byla tak akorát. Překvapilo mě, že se ani jednou neobjevily žádné saláty, bábovky atd.

The scrambled eggs were awful, the cakes that seemed homemade were also awful, some of the mousses in jars were disgusting, a greasy, tasteless goo.
I would expect much more from a 5-star hotel.
The room is dirty and not cleaned.

Overall, the stay was not very pleasant.

title: Old equipment - not a spa hotel
pos_text: The place is nice - next to a park and very friendly staff. Especially the experienced gentleman at the entrance who helps you with navigation.

I stayed in the king suite yet unfortunately the room was not in good shape. I have a video that the ceiling is dripping when you take a shower. The soap dispenser was not working properly and many things had damages. Again, for king suite I would expect a bit better quality - not that those things ruin everything. You can still have a nice sleep but for a king suite, I think it can be fixed a bit more often as it shows how much you care for your best experience.

For a hotel that has SPA in it, firstly it is tiny. Secondly, everyone was wearing swimming suit in the sauna and when I asked the super nice staff, they said - people are shy we started allowing - I think a hygiene issue should not be handled this way. Especially for a “spa hotel”

title: Disappointing

neg_text:
Another visit to this hotel and another unpleasant situation with incompetent reception staff. A deposit is charged which the lady at the reception simply forgot about. Despite asking twice, the woman simply acted as if she was in another world and did not know what was being said to her. When the deposit was finally returned, it seems to me that the room where my personal belongings were left, including my work phone, should have been checked first.
Shameful behavior for a 5-star hotel that no one informed me about finding it, as it turned out 10 minutes after my check-out that the phone had been left at the hotel so I could come back for it. Only late in the evening, when I was home, did I notice that I did not have my phone. I called the staff and asked if it had been found and that due to the incident with another guest, they simply "forgot to inform me". How can a hotel of this caliber and with accolades everywhere allow such situations to occur in 2026? Every visit there is the same problem with the young people at the reception. Does anyone managing the facility know about this?

The room in the photo is much better than in real life (well, it’s clear here, I’ve already suffered) it’s impossible to sleep, the tram depot opposite is incredibly noisy!!! There's always something cracking in the rooms 🤦‍♂️either I arrived at the wrong time or who knows ))) in general, I didn't like it all, so to speak 🙄 unfortunately.
I thought breakfast would fix everything, but alas, whoever wrote about breakfasts, it means you haven’t visited a 5* yet. I had to stay at a 3* and it was better by all criteria.
So, may the hotel administration forgive me, but alas 🤷‍♂️

Then the reception, where we waited 10 minutes for anyone to show up.
The refrigerator in the room was out of order, and in the bathroom, the shower was not fully enclosed, and water was dripping into the middle of the room.
Additionally, when we wanted to use the wellness area, there was no hotel staff at the reception in this part. We found a note telling us to report to the main reception if we wanted to use the SPA. Unfortunately, no one answered at the reception, so we went to the reception physically. When we arrived, the lady was clearly surprised that we had come, as the card supposedly only concerned scheduled massages and treatments. So we went back and got the towels ourselves. It's a bit strange that there's no hotel staff in the sauna or jacuzzi area, if only for safety reasons.
Overall, the organization is quite chaotic and for a five-star hotel there are quite a few shortcomings.

In the evening, despite asking at the reception, no one came to serve the bar and the same thing happened to us in the SPA area - no staff? During the short period we were at the hotel, they tried to charge us extra fees twice for non-existent services. You have to constantly monitor what the staff is doing, which is why we are disappointed with our stay.

Went into bar for a drink - no staff, so left for a meal at a restaurant without one.
Dog wandering around in breakfast in the morning - barking and yelping, at least the hot water was back so he probably washed his paws.
Charged for parking! What an insult .

title: Not a 5-star hotel, more like a 3-star hotel!

neg_text:
We arrived at the hotel a little earlier (1 hour) due to a cancelled client appointment. Check It was not possible; we were left sitting in the lobby for an hour with the explanation that the rooms were not ready.

When we saw the room, we wondered what had been done to clean it. (Pictures)
Dust in the corners and long black hairs in the sink.

The wellness area is very outdated; there are ant nests in the relaxation area.
When asked, staff said that they had already informed management, but nothing would be done (see pictures). One can also see from the picture of the sauna rules that things are being done extremely carelessly here.
When I asked to speak to the manager, I was informed that he was not in the building.
Shortly after my request, the ant nests were vacuumed up, but two hours later they were back!

The sauna is supposed to be usable until 10 pm. We were there at 3 pm, everything was fine, the sauna temperature was 90 degrees. When we wanted to go for another sauna session at 7 pm, the sauna heater was already switched off, and the sauna only had a temperature of 60 degrees!

neg_text: Gold earrings with diamonds were left in the apartment. Unfortunately, they were not found.

neg_text: The hotel certainly does not correspond to the five-star category. Either excellent 3 stars or 4 stars to average. The first room we were given smelled extremely of cigarettes. The second room was absolutely fine, except for the water temperature, which was not hot enough even at maximum - the overall bathroom design is very unfortunate, as the shower area is not closed and water sprays out. As for the wellness, unfortunately it was one of the worst we've been to - no privacy and overall not cozy and poorly designed. Furthermore, parking is extremely expensive, even though the location in Wrocław is very far from the center. For dog walkers - an abnormally expensive fee for a dog (5 kg). Even in Switzerland, a five-star hotel in Geneva wasn't that expensive. I'm happy to pay for quality and a great wellness center, but this hotel doesn't match the price or the rating with its amenities.
