It was by far the worst nights sleep I have ever had! Then the next morning I thought I would get up and go for breakfast and sit in the spa hoping my room would get cleaned and I could then go back to sleep … return back to room at 4pm to still not have been cleaned , had to call reception over-wise I don’t think it would of been done. Very disappointed with the whole place and will not be returning. Didn’t even get a message back from the hotel to acknowledge the whole situation or offer any sort of solution. Waited 24 hours for a message and didn’t get it so here I am … my advice for any one going to stay here find out if they are having a party etc and if they are do not stay there.

Więc buczało jakbyśmy mieli traktor w pokoju nie do wytrzymania przez całą noc, a i tak w apartamencie było zimno! Jak na 5 gwiazdkowy hotel to jakaś porażka. A co zrobiła obsługa? Dali nam 20 zł rabatu na parking!!!!

nie polecam tego miejsc jeśli ktoś chce się wyspać i zjeść po podróży.

title: This is not a hotel, its an event center. Hotel guests are not appreciated
pos_text: Room was good
neg_text: They treat u like 2nd level guests. They rent out entire hotel for events every single day. ( not just weekends) and refuse to serve any food during your stay.

title: Wroclaw 1 night
pos_text: Massage. It's hard to find anything more.
neg_text: Complete drama. I don't really know who gave it 5 stars. From the very beginning (basically before your stay) problems started… The massage appointments had to be rescheduled half an hour earlier because even though the spa is open until 10pm, the massage had to end at 9:30pm, which means I had to cut the massage in half because We came from the route straight for a massage. The lady at the reception, Maria or Daria, couldn't quite cope with the invoice. And I was informed that I would not receive an invoice, only when I asked to contact the manager, the invoice was made in 45 seconds. But it was possible! The rooms show signs of time, but are relatively comfortable. We were there in a very hot weather, so the air conditioning was not coping well. The breakfast was quite modest (it's hard to say it's five-star - I recommend going to a five-star hotel and checking what they serve). The same lady at the reception who refused to issue an invoice told me that we had to use the parking ticket until the end of our trip. Upon departure, it turned out that the ticket did not work. There was a traffic jam behind us (the parking lot is so narrow that it's difficult for a larger car to pass), despite the information that we should call the phone number, no one answered. We had to go to the reception and wait again, because when there is checkout in the morning, one slow person is enough. To sum up, the hotel might not have left such bad memories, but the service at every stage was dramatic. To be replaced. We didn't just use the restaurant… I'm afraid of what we would "find" there 🤣 if you have the option of choosing another hotel, even with four stars, I recommend choosing 4 stars.

title: big disappointment. the hotel was unfairly rated so high
pos_text: Beds - large, comfortable, we really slept well, which is a plus. And I guess that's all.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, I have a lot of reservations:
1. First and foremost - I am allergic, I chose this hotel primarily for the sauna, because I really need it during this period. At the reception I was informed that the sauna is available from 7 a.m. I was there at 8:30. the wellness area was not cleaned, the saunas were cold, and the jacuzzi too. I had to leave after an hour to make it to the meeting. By this time the sauna had heated up to 40 degrees, so I was unable to use this facility. Around 9:00 a.m. the cleaning lady came, washed the windows, and scrubbed the floor with a bucket. I understand that such things are the norm in public swimming pools, but not in a hotel of this class in the wellness area (!). You can't relax in such conditions. A huge minus and this situation alone makes me regret my choice and if I had to choose a hotel again, I would definitely not choose this one.
2. Breakfast was mediocre, typical hotel breakfast, weak coffee, dry cakes. There was prosecco available at the entrance, it looked like the staff were going to pour it but they probably didn't feel like it, it was in a cooler with water - melted ice, it was warm. The tables were not cleaned, we could feel crumbs from the previous guests under our hands. The gentleman from the service suggested dishes to order. We ordered poached eggs - they were prepared incorrectly, with way too much vinegar, it was impossible to eat. We went out to the terrace for coffee and dessert, but it was not cleaned up there either, and the umbrellas were not open despite the strong sun.
3. The service here was also a huge disappointment, unfortunately. I arrived with a pregnant woman, no one asked if they could help us, no one held any doors, no one even showed the slightest interest in us. When leaving, we were informed about the cost of 80 PLN for parking, which we had not received any information about beforehand, the person at the reception did not even ask how our stay was, basically took our cards, issued an invoice and said "Goodbye" without even a smile, anything, as if we were making a problem of taking advantage of the offer.

title: Not worth the price, dirty bathroom and terrible service, as if they were working as a punishment, hidden fees
pos_text: The hotel is nice because it is stylish and nicely restored, and that is where the positives of this hotel end
neg_text: Employee approach to customer service. The gentleman working at the reception and the lady behind the bar gave me the impression that they were working there as a punishment. On the first day we were given a room without windows but with the name Anna, just as we wanted. The bathroom seemed to be not cleaned and there were remnants of cosmetics from the previous owner, which is documented in the photos when I asked the receptionist to move or upgrade to another room. He said that these rooms were not available. I was very surprised because the rooms were still available on booking.com, but due to the late hour, we didn't make much of a fuss. Suddenly the next day another employee upgraded us without any problem. When we wanted to use the bar, the lady said the bar was closed, and the gentleman at the reception who checked us in also said the same, even though there were people sitting inside.30 minutes later the bar was suddenly open and I could order a beer when I saw other guests ordering too. Work as punishment? It might be worth training employees. On the first evening we reported a problem with hot water because after one person took a bath it turned out that there was no hot water. The next morning the same problem also appeared and we reported the problem to the reception. On the second night after moving to another room, the problem with the water continued. Sometimes it was hot, sometimes it wasn't. We reported it several times to the reception desk, everyone pretended that everything was fine. The problem only resolved on Monday after check-out, when the receptionist admitted that they were right that they had a problem with the hot water. It's a shame that for two days, none of the employees were able to notice that cold water was running from the tap in every room. The pool area was very nice, but there was no one to ask about massages or additional spa services. Apparently this is a spa and wellness hotel?

title: Never again in this hotel.
neg_text: The worst breakfast (or rather lack thereof) I've seen in a five-star hotel - not only was the selection very limited, but hot dishes were only brought at 7:22. Unfortunately we had to leave and hardly used anything. Breakfast from 07:00 When I reported that more than half of the dishes were missing, the lady at the reception said that she UNDERSTOOD. Nothing more than a simple SORRY. NEVER AGAIN PLATINUM PALACE BOUTIQUE HOTEL in Wroclaw.

title: I'm not satisfied, I won't go back there again.
pos_text: red carpet in the hallway
neg_text: Monotonous breakfast, uncomfortable tables, you can't open the window in the room, small SPA, non-functioning air conditioning, VERY EXPENSIVE parking

title: Bad
neg_text: Two days before arrival (stay 14-16/12) the hotel informed me that the restaurant would be closed "only on 15/12". After a 5.5-hour train journey, I found out that the restaurant was also open on 14/12: "only open from 9 p.m. to 10 p.m.". Laughter in the hall… As compensation I was offered "free parking" (I clearly informed the hotel that I was arriving by train). They clearly favor holiday parties at the expense of individual guests. Supreme service is completely unknown to them. The lady at the reception doesn't know anything because "she wasn't there yesterday." Never again in this place.

title: Historic hotel, cool decor
pos_text: Central location
neg_text: Breakfast was expensive

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Noise in the room, probably from some air conditioning system, and the noise from a jackhammer or impact drill all day long.

title: I will definitely not return to this hotel
pos_text:
Very good cuisine!!!
Breakfast prefect - nothing to add, nothing to take away.
neg_text: The room is dark, gloomy and above all noisy. You can hear everything from the corridor and the bar located on the ground floor, as well as from the room next door.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: spa
neg_text: cleanliness

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Location
neg_text: Room 504 is a terrible room, the curtains are impossible to close. The middle section of the sunscreens was not working. No reading light next to the bed. With the light switch next to the bed you can only turn the light off, not on. This room is not 5 star worthy.

title: It was a rather pleasant stay.
pos_text: Beautiful location, great breakfast
neg_text: The hotel is very dark in its decor; it's somewhat unusual to be observed through the glass partition in the room while walking.

title: Passable
pos_text: The proximity to the city center. The grilled and raw vegetables at breakfast, the pastes. But 5 stars? Never
neg_text: It was too dark for me, almost eerie, and couldn't be changed by switching all the light switches on at once. There is too much bare concrete and steel instead of any reminder of the former monastery. Nevertheless, the signs of wear and tear were clearly visible in the damp corners of the house. The gym lacked lat pulldown machines or cable pulleys. Nespresso coffee cannot compete with any good coffee machine and is also a terrible environmental killer. The lady at reception during check-in was very polite and tried hard, but unfortunately also heartlessly arrogant. Luckily, it was only one; all the others were genuinely friendly!

title: Very impersonal
neg_text: I have been coming to this hotel every year for 10 years, but every time I am treated like a stranger. This time I was even threatened with cancellation, strange...

title: Passable
pos_text: Great location. Nice staff. Very comfortable bed
neg_text:
View from the window on the cathedral wall - ok. Such a location. But because of this, the rooms could use more light.
The bathroom window is leaky, so the bathroom is cold. The decor is very austere. A matter of taste.
Certainly, since the hotel has a reputation for being the best in the area... Hmm

title: Passable
pos_text: The room is very large and the bed is comfortable
neg_text: The pool and jacuzzi have cold water, so you can't relax in the jacuzzi at this temperature, I was disappointed

title: Dissapointing - certainly not what I was expecting for the price I paid
pos_text:
The pool and spa area was nice .

neg_text: The curtains don’t close properly , they are on a wire. The shower is ridiculous , the hands free holder is rather low and the water flys out the screen area . The atmosphere is dark and aged in the room we were in - definite only a 3 star property . It needs a renovation, the paint on our ceiling was peeling and walls scuffed and marked . Terrible lighting

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The place is close to the center
neg_text: Late night noise from inside the hotel. I think they were moving stuff or something. We couldn't sleep.

title: TV only Polish and weak lighting
pos_text: Nice big room and exceptional bathroom and lovely pool. Staff was friendly and actually the stay was ok, but see below
neg_text:
1. dim lighting. For that price, there could also be light.
2. No decent international TV programs. Only Polish and Italian and RTL
3. loud short noises in the middle of the night
4. Only one blanket for 2

title: Good location, friendly service and lovely cocktails! However, a peaceful spa moment is not guaranteed!
pos_text: Friendly staff and top location! The hotel's rooftop bar was cozy and had the best drinks, so I highly recommend the rooftop bar!
neg_text: SPA. Unfortunately, the spa level did not meet expectations for a 5-star hotel. The pool was full of people and families with children and there were only a few beds next to the pool, so we really couldn't fit in there and didn't get to swim on the first day. I think the spa's standard is lowered by the fact that people outside the hotel can also buy a visit for cheap. People also didn't shower before going into the pool.

title: The hotel does not live up to its 5 stars
pos_text:
The fruit selection at breakfast was outstanding.
The soundproof windows saved our night's sleep.
neg_text:
Hardly any bread selection at breakfast. There was only one!!! Salad and no cream cheese for breakfast. We would have expected more in a 5-star hotel.
In our room, we had the doorknob in our hand when closing the bathroom door and the window handle when opening the window! Without words...
Behind the armchairs in the room hadn't been cleaned for a long time.
What a shame!

title: Correct
pos_text: Good location and nice roof terrace
neg_text: Terribly poor service, rooftop restaurant advertises with Michelin and there is a disaster. The waiters can't remember orders. I asked for the menu three times and then they said they didn't know if there would be a table for me. How can they not know such things!!! Besides, they don't see the customer, you have to follow them and argue. The biggest minus of this hotel!!!

title: First impression WOW! Thereafter: NEVER AGAIN!!!
pos_text: This hotel is a true classic – in the restaurant and foyer you can feel the charm of the good old days. The wellness area also impresses with great offers. The situation couldn't be better. The design and features take some getting used to, but are very memorable. The breakfast is very plentiful; The selection is limited, but the quality of the products is good. I spent my birthday there and received a fruit basket and a birthday greeting—a very nice gesture.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, I was not satisfied with my stay. The check-in process was very reserved and unfriendly – guests should have felt much more welcome!
The first room was a disaster: The air conditioner hummed so loudly that the bed vibrated all night. Directly behind our bed was the bathroom in the next room – as soon as the tap was turned on, we were awake. I have never experienced such noise, even in old houses.
Additionally, our safe had a loose connection and was therefore unusable. We reported this to reception – they promised to take care of it, but nothing happened. The first night was terrible. We've never stayed in such a noisy hotel before—it was more like a youth hostel.
The next morning we mentioned it again and luckily we quickly got a new room. As an apology, we were invited to breakfast. The gentleman at the reception was very friendly.
The new room was much quieter, and the air conditioning and safe worked perfectly. There was even a bathtub with a whirlpool function – but it was broken, which was almost amusing. 5*??? At least we were finally able to sleep well.
The wellness area is generally nice and the staff there are very friendly. However, you shouldn’t look too closely: A strip of ledge is missing at the pool for about ten meters – presumably fallen off and never repaired. The entire area looks dirty, and there is extensive mold on the ceiling, especially above the whirlpool. The dim light conceals the whole thing a little. Be careful in the whirlpool: The stone tiles are so chipped in some places that there is a risk of injury. In addition, the loungers in the pool area are rusting badly—we ruined our bathrobes.
By the way, when we checked out, we weren't asked how we liked our stay – which is unfortunately somewhat telling.

title: Nice location ….staff need training
pos_text: Location wax good….nice building and spa
neg_text: Staff in the restaurant and bar were poor….we would wait 15 minutes to have our order taken….coffee was from a machine u would have at home….not a real 5 star in my opinion

title: Passable
pos_text: Location
neg_text:
Actually, the hotel can't provide appropriate services according to 5-star hotel.
You can hear everything from the neighboring rooms.

title: Dark
pos_text:
Overall, the stay was quite good. Unfortunately, the pictures are somewhat misleading. It was not nearly as bright and friendly as it is portrayed here. It's rather oppressive. Rooms, lobby, corridors and also the spa area.
The only really positive thing was the beds. They were super comfortable.

title: It's even cool
pos_text: Terrace and spa area
neg_text: Cleaning service driving a trolley from the very morning - which makes it impossible to sleep

title: Needs renovation or price reduction
pos_text: Location right next to the market square. Swimming pool.
neg_text:
The hotel is old and dark, and charges high fees for everything. Definitely not worth the price.
Breakfasts are terrible, scrambled eggs made from powder. Staff is very young and poorly trained.

title: Passable
pos_text: Intimate swimming pool, salt cave, cleanliness
neg_text: Room acoustics. We spent two nights there and two nights we didn't sleep. You can hear absolutely everything from other rooms. From conversations, through flushing the toilet or taking a bath. In addition, from time to time something humming would turn on. Air conditioning or a furnace? And on the second night the cleaning ladies started vacuuming before 8 a.m., which, given the acoustics, woke us up from bed because the sound was so loud that I thought someone was vacuuming in our room... A specific window. At a height of about 150 cm with a view of the wall.

title: Passable
pos_text:
Jacuzzi with cold water. Usually, a person wants to relax in hot water with bubbles, but here the temperature is like in a swimming pool.
Small selection for breakfast, e.g. one type of ham, if someone doesn't like a particular ham, they won't eat it anymore. The list of coffee capsules includes 10 items, of which 3 are available, two of which are not even on the list, so it is not known what strength they have.
We had a deluxe room... The mattress was terrible, soft and definitely needed replacing. Two nights and two mornings we woke up with back pain, completely sleepless, the toilet glass was completely nonsensical... I understand that it might be nice to watch my wife bathe while lying on the bed, but it's not very comfortable to sit on the toilet when another person can see us from the bed, the sound insulation of the rooms is terrible... When the lady next door was taking a shower, we couldn't hear the TV at all. I run a construction company and whoever built these rooms probably didn't do a good job. The pipes are completely without insulation, as are the walls between the rooms, and the floors and ceilings are also poorly insulated because you can hear everyone opening the curtains on a metal cord above the rad, which is another weak point. The curtains are so high that they are difficult to move, there should be a stick with a hook or something next to them so that you can grab the wheels and move them, not to mention that it would be enough to install electric curtains with a button. The hotel can probably afford such things, as another thing that strikes me as offensive is the irrationally high price of the products in the mini bar... Starting with the fact that in some five-star hotels, products that are already available in the bar are free of charge or, if they are paid, they are simply not available and are made to order. And here the fridge is so full that you can hardly put anything of your own in it and the price card is pushed on the top of the fridge for e.g. Peanuts with prices like Red Bull for 25 PLN or peanuts for 25 PLN. The only positive thing about staying at the hotel was the really nice and pleasant service. Gentlemen and ladies are always happy to help and always smiling.

title: There is nothing special, there are no basic amenities
pos_text: Unfortunately, the room lacked basic equipment such as a kettle, coffee, tea... Which seems to be standard in 2+ hotels. The room itself was spacious, nothing fancy. No iron, ironing board. Smart TV also does not work. I've stayed in many better hotels with fewer stars.

title: Minimalism when it comes to 5 stars. Many hotels with a penny rating do a better job.
pos_text: Architecture
neg_text: Lighting in the room (no light in the room), cold water in the jacuzzi and pool, no kettle in the room.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: Cold water in the jacuzzi

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The hotel was great, but the guests were a disaster.
The sauna was loud, the guests had no manners, and no understanding of sauna culture.
Drinking and smoking in the relaxation area and by the pool.
And the hotel was very noisy at night.
Breakfast was very good, which is to be expected for 800 zloti for 6 breakfasts.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: I had the pleasure of having breakfast at the hotel and I am very pleased! The choice of dishes is wide – fresh bread, delicious cheeses, cold meats, fruit, as well as warm options that were perfectly prepared. Everything is fresh and tasty, and the service is extremely polite and helpful. The pleasant atmosphere makes every morning start really well here. I definitely recommend it!
neg_text: The stay at the hotel was not entirely comfortable. The beds were too soft, which made sleeping on them uncomfortable and I woke up sore. It would definitely be nice to be able to choose firmer mattresses. Additionally, the prices in the hotel bar are definitely too high. This makes using the bar not a pleasant addition, but rather an unnecessary expense. The hotel has its advantages, but these issues need improvement.

title: Hotel 5* rozczarowujacy standardem.
pos_text: Super lokalizacja. Piekna, duza lazienka. Swietne sauny.
neg_text: Zimno w pokoju, poduszka jakby polowy wkladu nie bylo, jeden szampon, jeden zel pod prysznic do noclegu dla dwoch osob. Brak czajnika/zestawu do zaparzenia kawy. Woda w basenie i jacuzzi tak zimna ze nie da sie wejsc. Poplamione wykladziny na korytarzu.

title: Viel zu teuer. Keine eigenen PP. Minibar zu Teuer.
pos_text:
Das Hotel war in der nähe vom Stadt Zentrum.
Der Checkin Verlief sehr Schnell.
Das Bett hätte etwas grösser sein können.
Die Bettwäsche waren sauber.
Das wir nur 2 Nächte da waren hat uns gereicht um zu erkennen das der Preis übberteuert ist.
(291€)
neg_text:
Die Duschbrause spritzte das wasser durch den raum, so war der boden am eingang vom bad immer nass.
Der Versteller von der badewanne war Defekt. So konnten wir nur mit tricks die badewanne benutzen.
Auf den Bilder vom Bad sehen sie Pflegeprodukte von Rituals. Dem war leider nicht so.
Es gab keine Hoteleigene PP. Wir musten auserhalb Parken.
Die Preise der Minibar waren Viel Teurer wie bei uns in der Schweiz.

title: The hotel does not live up to its rating.
pos_text: The only positive aspect I can mention is the spa – the pool and sauna were excellent.
neg_text:
First of all, one of the water bottles in the room was already opened when we checked in. The seal on the cap clearly indicated that the bottle had been opened, and it was filled to the brim with tap water.
After we took a bath, we couldn’t drain the water because the valve was broken. I had to deal with it myself, as we didn’t want to contact the hotel on our special day – we just wanted to relax.
In the double room, there was only one set of towels, bathrobes, and slippers. We had to request an additional set for the second guest.
We stayed on the second floor, and there was some kind of corporate event happening on the first floor, which made it very difficult to rest. The music was extremely loud. After midnight, we reported it to the reception, but the music only stopped closer to 1 a.m.

It felt more like a standard 4-star hotel located in an old historic building.

title: Overrated hotel
pos_text:
Good location, late check-out.
Warm beds. Large bathroom.
neg_text:
Windows poorly soundproofed. The cleaning staff did not understand any English at all, so there were problems arranging the time for room cleaning. That's probably why my children's room wasn't cleaned. Sauna quite small (just enough room for 6 people to sit).
The agreed price in euros on Booking.com increased by approximately 6% on the final hotel bill: Reason for exchange rate fluctuation. The exchange rate has not changed by 6% since booking 1 month ago. We traveled a lot in the USA this year, and we never had a situation like this there.

title: Passable
pos_text: Great location and tasty breakfast
neg_text: The room has a cold floor (bare panels - unpleasant and loud, amplifying all noises), which made it very loud. A centimeter gap between the door and the corridor - every noise and every conversation in the corridor can be heard in the room. It is completely impossible to sleep or rest in such conditions. There is no kettle or coffee maker in the room. The design of the shower causes a large puddle to form in the bathroom. Overall, I do not recommend it - for this price you can definitely find better hotels.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Style, history of the object
neg_text: I chose the hotel because of the saunas and swimming pool to relax in the evening. The pool was cold and overcrowded, the jacuzzi had the same water - very cold, the dry sauna was broken, the wet one turned off after 5 minutes and never turned back on. No relaxation

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Very good SPA facility, very good location.
neg_text: Poor staff service, very poor Standard room equipment, no parking (very difficult to find a space). There are much better options in Wrocław at this price.

title: A very successful stay
pos_text: Breakfast is average, location is ok
neg_text: Appearance of the rooms

title: Beautiful on the outside, needs renovation on the inside
pos_text: Location and beautiful building from the OUTSIDE
neg_text: The inside of the building needs refreshing. No sockets by the bed, no possibility of making a frosted glass in the bathroom. The curtains are difficult to open and close - these are probably the biggest disadvantages

title: This hotel does not deserve 5*.
pos_text: I liked the building and the historic stairs inside. Great location.
neg_text: When I first wake up, the room is filled with battered furniture and a stained armchair. There is mold in the bathroom shower. The flush cistern is broken. The rain shower on the ceiling is in terrible condition. The carpets in the hallway are stained.The noise is terrifying, you can hear everything from the corridor. The elevator is terribly slow, better to take the stairs. Standard breakfast. I might even say it's a poor choice. After reporting my concerns, they changed us to a room with a higher standard. It was definitely quieter and larger. However, the shower was not without mold either.

title: We simply won't come back.
pos_text: We returned to the hotel because of the location, proximity to the guarded parking lot, the hotel design and the great breakfast and good service so far. remaining location and design of the hotel.
neg_text: Being in the hotel again, we were given a room that was worn out by time, we were treated as 1 person - 1 bathrobe, 1 set of towels, 1 slippers - we returned late so we gave up calling the room service. The last straw was the bathtub, which I gave up on in the morning when I remembered getting into it in the evening. Even my very capable husband had trouble, and I will add that there was no handle installed next to the bathtub, which is a requirement in facilities categorized below. When I passed this comment on to the receptionist I heard "the handles do not match the design of the building's owners"w-well, my hands are at my feet. And breakfast? I know many 4-star hotels of a much higher standard.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: waiters, very amateur behavior and service for a 5* hotel

title: Location is good, but the hotel is not 5 stars in my opinion.
pos_text: The location
neg_text:
For a 5-star hotel, the room was very spartan!
There were no coffee or tea facilities, the bathroom is large but very illogically laid out, the lighting was also mediocre, and the bed was much too low and uncomfortable.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The hotel has a very central location; everything is easily accessible. The breakfast was not worth the price paid.
The pool and sauna are good, but the whirlpool was not working, nor was the whirlpool bath in the room.
When we complained, the hotel employee said that almost all the bathtubs were defective. The doors in our rooms could not be locked from the inside either. Oh well
neg_text:
The service is simply bad, there's constant crashing because someone drops cutlery or plates, the worst was on the roof terrace, where the waiter knocked over the drinks on the table while serving. He then came with a dry cloth and some cellulose and wiped it evenly across the tabletop.
So he left it at that and never came back. We went there.
I would have expected more for €7.50 for a lemonade.

title: Passable
pos_text: The location is a huge plus and this is probably the main reason for the price, but it is not 5 stars. We've stayed in many five-star hotels and this one is nothing like that. Poor and very poor room decor, only basic toiletries in the bathroom, no iron. This is not what we expected from a 5-star hotel.
neg_text: Nice staff. Location

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Sauna, swimming pool, hammam. Stylization.
neg_text: The air conditioning was making noise at night. The shower sink was clogged and later repaired.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Facilities are great
neg_text: My bathroom shower clogged badly. Wasn't expecting that from a 5 star

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location, delicious breakfast. The gym is great for breathing, even though it's small, the salt on the wall and the aroma really clear the respiratory tract. The salt cave is wonderful. The reception was polite, the gentleman helped us a lot in ordering a taxi to the concert.
neg_text: No tea and coffee making facilities in the room, I had to ask for a kettle. Housekeeping definitely needs improvement - the ladies came only after 4 p.m., I had to leave the room on purpose :/ they only changed the towels and made the beds, but they didn't fold the clothes, didn't refill the mineral water, took away my tea cup and my private tea strainer. At the Acquaria restaurant the food was rather average, but the service was very slow. Room service has no items for children apart from bolognese, I asked for plain yellow cheese and tomato with bread and butter, but I didn't receive it. I was convinced that Likus concept was a brand where everything was impeccable, but I was very disappointed with this hotel. Next time definitely Puro!

So entering the room was a struggle that must have taken a few seconds :)

title: A good hotel in a very good location, but it doesn't quite live up to its own expectations.

neg_text: There is only one elevator; sometimes you have to wait 5-10 minutes before you can get in. Breakfast policy is very arbitrary and confusing; during the week coffee is served at the table, but on weekends you have to get it yourself. No menu for breakfast extras such as omelettes etc. will be available on weekends. At breakfast, even on the fourth time by the same person, they ask you in excruciating detail what your room number is and what your exact name is. Many show-offs stop here with their powerful and loud vehicles, revving their engines arbitrarily, especially during quiet hours at night, and disturbing the guests without being addressed by the hotel staff.

neg_text: Unfortunately, the service was abysmal. We celebrated a birthday in the main restaurant (which is extremely expensive for Poland). The food was really good, but unfortunately the service was completely impersonal and cold. Afterwards, we wanted to move to the (half empty) roof terrace for desserts and drinks at half past eight, where we were turned away extremely rudely by the head waiter on the grounds that they no longer had any clean glasses. Actually, the bar upstairs is open until 11 pm, there were numerous free tables and plenty of staff. As "compensation," after I complained at reception, a bottle of sparkling wine was brought to our room at 10 p.m., unfortunately without a friendly word, an apology, or even a birthday greeting. We're not usually squeamish, but we felt really badly treated here. Unfortunately, the service was not commensurate with the level, price, and excessive staffing of the establishment.

The staff while trying their best also did not satisfy, the rooftop bar could not welcome us on an evening after rainy day as they did not wipe the tables clean and we were not allowed to sit in the restaurant part to have drinks. We went to a bar right next to the hotel where we sat outside on dry chairs and enjoyed the cooler air.

Hotel staff attire: gentlemen in baggy suits, ladies in sexist aprons straight from the Polish People's Republic.
The rooms are large but cold, empty, with terrible curtains.

Terribly cold pool, cold water jacuzzi, rusty, cheap sunbeds, dark, cold. Terrible SPA zone.

Evening treat: a bag of nuts. Leave it to yourself :)

No kettle, coffee or tea in the room. When we asked for the order, of course only two teas were brought - so that we wouldn't accidentally drink more.

It's also worth checking out this place's Instagram and hashtags - the ladies advertising themselves in every other photo are a perfect testament to the quality of this place.

The windows don't open, so you have to leave the air conditioning on all the time (it worked, but it was drafty), and the beds are narrow and uncomfortable. The staff at reception were unfriendly. During our absence, the room was entered, obviously to affix a sticker, and the peel-off film was left in the bathroom. The cleaning staff knocked us out at 9 a.m. In some places at the rear of the large parking lot, the barriers are missing, so that with some effort it would be possible to drive a car out of the parking lot without authorization. Parking fee overpriced, overall poor value for money. There is currently a large construction site right next to the hotel. Although the hotel is not directly near the train station, there are sex shops on the next street. We left a day early so we wouldn't have to spend another night there. At least we were reimbursed for one day’s parking fee.

neg_text: Our main criteria was to be close to the train station and have a quiet stay. After reading the reviews, during the booking I specified that we'd like rooms on the quiet side. When we arrived, I again reiterated this request to the lady at the reception. She looked at our reservation and said yes, here are your rooms 323 and 325. I don't know if the other rooms are even noisier, but these are not quiet. Moreover, the sound isolation between the rooms is also poor. Either way, for us it was a disappointing experience.

title: Poor
pos_text: Location, cleanliness
neg_text: Extremely dry air in the room, poorly functioning elevators or not working at all

title: Poor
pos_text: The hotel is well-kept and the room I stayed in was spacious and, above all, very clean.
neg_text: The stay at this hotel turned out to be very disappointing. There was no electricity in the room from midnight, which made it impossible to work in the morning. I reported the fault, but despite assurances, the service did not take any action to solve the problem. I asked to speak to the shift manager and after speaking with him, I was assured that he would contact me by phone. Unfortunately, I didn't live to see it. This approach is unprofessional and disrespectful towards the guest. I expected a much better level of service and commitment with such a number of positive reviews and the price.

title: We were unable to stay overnight and were looking for another accommodation option despite the amount paid (
pos_text: The hotel service is clear and pleasant. Friendly reception staff.
neg_text: There is no possibility to open the windows. The air conditioning and ventilation system are old. The air is humid and "heavy." Plus flavoring…

title: Okay
pos_text: It’s a great location near the city centre
neg_text: The room wasn’t as good as expected by visiting other Ibis hotels around Poland. The toilet hasn’t been topped up (toilet paper was left half used by previous visitors etc) and it wasn’t as clean as I would want it

title: As above

neg_text:
VERY DIRTY CARPETS IN THE ROOM. BRR.
RATHER NOT FOR WASHING BUT FOR REPLACEMENT.

I PAID ALMOST 900 PLN FOR TWO NIGHTS. I WAS THINKING ABOUT A HIGHER STANDARD. BUT THIS IS MY SUBJECTIVE OPINION. MAYBE I'M WRONG. MAYBE FOR 900 PLN IT MAY BE DIRTY. BTW. I'M NOT MEAN, BUT HOW CAN YOU SUPPORT CLEANERS WHO, INSTEAD OF IDENTIFYING THEM WITH THEIR WORKPLACE, SIMPLY IGNORE THEIR DUTIES? I DO NOT MEAN A MOMENTARY BREAK. IT TOOK MUCH LONGER. I AM A PRIORITY MYSELF AND I DO NOT AGREE TO SUCH INSUBORDINATION. MY OPINION IS IN NO CASE INTENDED TO OFFEND ANYONE. JUST TO INCREASE THE PRESTIGE OF YOUR HOTEL. SO MUCH.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Breakfast and children's corner
neg_text: Room missing fridge and wine glasses...Dirty windows...few towels

title: disappointing experience
pos_text: Location is good, breakfast is nice for a 3* hotel, room was clean
neg_text:
You cannot open the window. There is no option to get a fresh air. Only option is AC unit. Air in the room was the same as after longhaul airplane trip - unplesant, dry and stale. If you are sensitive to this, be aware. No ventilation on toilet. AC is noisy, so earplugs are the must.

There was only one duvet/bed cover for two people. We asked the staff multiple (!!!) times during the stay, if it is possible to get another one - they said yes but never delivered.

(Two) pillows were different size, both uncomfortable.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: It was clean and the staff were really nice and l also got my VAT receipt
neg_text: Facilities were not so good, the bathroom was too small, the tea bags were not added throughout the stay, the wardrobe and tv were really small.

title: Terrible experience
pos_text: Receptionist was nice
neg_text:
No heating with minus three degrees outside.
The staff sent two portable heaters to heat the room and the power went out.
As a result, I get a fever of 39.

title: Very bad stay
pos_text: The only thing we liked was the location within walking distance of the old town and the sights.
neg_text:
The room was clean but there was a very significant problem: the window did not open and there was no possibility of ventilation, which caused us a very unpleasant feeling during our stay.
Also, the service at the reception was not helpful at all, we didn't understand English well when we asked some of our questions and the employee reacted inappropriately and laughed ironically at each other.
The pillows are quite small, the towels are carpets, you need improvement in both the service and the room amenities.
Breakfast is average with few choices.
They also sent us a message a day before we left asking us to give them a rating of 9 or 10 and if we had any complaints, to report it to the reception and not to leave a negative comment with a low rating.

title: This is the worst Ibis hotel we have ever stayed at
pos_text: Unfortunately nothing!
neg_text: Where should one even begin? Ibis has apparently bought some rooms from Novotel? Unfortunately everything is bad! The breakfast was just ridiculous. Breakfast room in a corner of the hotel, separate from Novotel. The bed seemed clean, but unfortunately the skin started to itch. We booked 3 nights, but checked out after the second night. The manager was extremely helpful and tried to give us a new room. But that was out of the question for us. We received full reimbursement for parking and one night's stay.

title: Tragedy. It's a waste of money.
pos_text: - LACK
neg_text:
- I received a room for disabled people, Mr.128 (I didn't book one). The sink was at knee level.
- broken bed (couldn't sleep)
- carpet with stains (diameter 40 cm)
- dirt in the bathroom
- worn, old towels
- rude, arrogant service (The lady "rolled her eyes" when I said I had to go to the parking lot to get my car ticket)
- parking fee: PLN 60/day
- from the parking lot you have to walk up the hill with your suitcases to the hotel. Small number of parking spaces.
I have never encountered such a low standard in Ibis/Novotel hotels in Poland or abroad.
If it weren't for my late arrival, I would have left this hotel. I have the impression that the staff deliberately gave me the worst room because I came with my dog.. Other reviews do not indicate such a tragic condition of the hotel. Maybe it's company policy.

title: Bad

title: Unacceptable stay!
neg_text: Room without opening the window, locked up like mice. There were no face towels, we asked for them to give us some. A basket in the bathroom without a lid, no basket anywhere else in the room. No refrigerator!!! and a thin table where you could only put a few things. Expensive accommodation (90€ per night) and the room unacceptable!

title: Very poor
neg_text: The bed wasn't comfortable; I ended up with lower back pain. It's a shame this happened to us. In the other Ibis hotels we stayed in, the beds were very comfortable. Here, they had put some kind of futon on top of the bed—not comfortable at all.
